title: rubus canadensis var. millspaughii
intro: Rubus canadensis var. millspaughii, also known as the black raspberry, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and antiseptic qualities, which can help to treat wounds, sore throats, and digestive issues. The berries of the black raspberry plant have been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. Black raspberry herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and jams, have been used to support overall health and well-being, and are often consumed for their potential anti-cancer and anti-aging effects.
